title: 10 medicinal constituents of Atractylodes macrocephala (white atractylodes)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Atractylodes macrocephala inulin is a type of polysaccharide extracted from its rhizome. It has been widely used in traditional Chinese medicine for its health-promoting properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Inulin has also been shown to exhibit prebiotic effects, stimulating the growth of beneficial gut bacteria and improving digestion. Additionally, it is believed to have potential applications in the prevention and treatment of various diseases, such as cardiovascular disease and cancer.

Atractylodes macrocephala atractylonides is a type of rhizome that belongs to the family Compositae. It is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its numerous health benefits. The plant contains compounds such as atructurin, atractylenolide, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.

Atractylodes macrocephala beta-eudesmol is a bioactive compound isolated from the rhizome of this plant. It has been widely used in traditional Chinese medicine for its therapeutic effects on various diseases, including bronchitis, asthma, and inflammatory bowel disease. Beta-eudesmol exhibits anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory activities, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. Its chemical structure is similar to that of eudesmol, another bioactive compound found in Atractylodes macrocephala.

Atractylodes macrocephala alpha-hederin is a bioactive compound isolated from the rhizome of Atractylodes macrocephala, a traditional Chinese herb. It has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive activities, making it a potential treatment for various diseases such as arthritis, lupus, and other autoimmune disorders. Alpha-hederin has also been found to have anticancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis. Its mechanisms of action involve modulation of immune cell function and inhibition of pro-inflammatory signaling pathways.

Atractylodes macrocephala taraxasterol is a type of triterpenoid saponin that is isolated from the roots and rhizomes of Atractylodes macrocephala Koidz. It has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various diseases such as bronchitis, asthma, and skin infections.

Atractylodes macrocephala betulinic acid is a triterpenoid saponin isolated from the rhizomes of Atractylodes macrocephala. It has been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antitumor activities, making it a promising agent for the treatment of various diseases. Additionally, betulinic acid has been found to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis (programmed cell death) in certain tumor types.

Atractylodes macrocephala 3-O-acetyl-alpha-eudesmol is a bioactive compound isolated from the rhizome of Atractylodes macrocephala. It has been found to possess various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, it has been shown to exhibit cytotoxicity against certain cancer cell lines, making it a promising candidate for further research in the development of new medicines.

Atractylodes macrocephala alpha-amyrin is a triterpenoid saponin extracted from the root of this plant species. It has been found to possess various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. Studies have shown that alpha-amyrin exhibits inhibitory activity against enzymes involved in protein glycosylation, making it a potential therapeutic agent for treating diseases associated with protein misfolding.

Atractylodes macrocephala unsolic acid is a bioactive compound isolated from its roots. It has been shown to possess various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Unsolic acid has also been found to have potential therapeutic effects on cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular diseases, making it a promising natural product for the development of new medicines.

Atractylodes macrocephala oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid saponin extracted from the rhizome of Atractylodes macrocephala Koidz. It has been extensively studied for its biological and pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties. Oleanolic acid has also been shown to possess hepatoprotective and cardioprotective effects, making it a promising natural compound for the treatment of various diseases.

intro: Atractylodes macrocephala has active constituents such as sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and saponins. These compounds confer anti-inflammatory, antidiabetic, and antimicrobial properties to the plant, making it a valuable herb in traditional Chinese medicine. The sesquiterpenes, for instance, have been shown to reduce blood sugar levels, while the flavonoids exhibit antioxidant activity that can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. By harnessing these medicinal properties, white atractylodes has been used to treat conditions such as diabetes, respiratory infections, and digestive disorders, improving overall health and well-being for many people around the world.
intro_desc: Atractylodes macrocephala has active constituents such as atractylon, atractylenolide, and atractyloside. These constituents are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. They can help improve lives by reducing symptoms of respiratory infections and fever, as well as alleviating conditions such as rheumatism and arthritis. By incorporating white atractylodes into their treatment plans, individuals can experience relief from discomfort and improve their overall quality of life.
